column1: # mapping_set_title: 'OCSF to CISA KEV Catalog class mappings'
#   Class-level mappings between OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework)
#   classes and the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog schema.
#   KEV entries represent CVEs confirmed as exploited in the wild, mandating
#   federal remediation timelines under BOD 22-01. OCSF vulnerability and CVE
#   objects cover the same vulnerability domain; EPSS provides the probabilistic
#   exploit score that contextualises KEV membership.

column8: OCSF Cve models any CVE record; KevEntry is the subset confirmed exploited in the wild — every KevEntry has a corresponding CVERecord but not vice versa.
OCSF Vulnerability wraps CVE references and may refer to a KEV-listed entry; close match because KEV is a curated subset of the broader vulnerability population.
A vulnerability finding event for a KEV-listed CVE directly corresponds to a KevEntry; findings for non-KEV CVEs are similar in structure but out of KEV scope.
EPSS probability scores contextualise exploitation likelihood; KEV membership confirms active exploitation — related but distinct signals for the same vulnerability.
KEV entries include a required remediation due-date; OCSF Remediation describes the recommended fix — related through the shared remediation mandate.
